title: rubus adulterinus
intro: Rubus adulterinus, also known as the false raspberry, is a species of flowering plant in the rose family that is native to the Himalayas and parts of Southeast Asia. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rubus adulterinus include reducing fever, relieving pain and inflammation, and treating digestive issues, as well as its potential to lower blood sugar levels and improve cardiovascular health. Rubus adulterinus is typically prepared as a tea or tincture, with the dried leaves and roots being steeped in hot water to make a medicinal infusion.
